title: Amendments
lead: In a parliamentary democracy limited by the Constitution, the extent to which it can be amended determines a delicate balance between ==parliamentary sovereignty== and ==constitutional supremacy==.

items: The power to amend is ==constituent power== — exercised specially by Parliament under ==Art 368==, not under the legislative division of powers in the VII Schedule.
The Constitution places ==no explicit restrictions== on Parliament's amending power — it was ==judicial intervention== that created the Doctrine of Basic Structure to impose principle-based limits.
Art 368 is a ==confluence of world constitutions== — US, Ireland, Australia.

items.title: Procedure
bullets: Introduced in ==either House== (LS = RS, no joint sitting)

items.title: Without State Ratification
bullets: For provisions that are ==unitary== in character

items.title: With State Ratification
bullets: Special majority + ratification by ==half the States== (simple majority)

sections.title: Why Art 368 Matters — Should Power Be Restricted — How

blocks.text: The power to amend the Constitution is a solemn responsibility, not a tool of convenience. In a nation as plural and aspirational as India, this power must be exercised not merely through the arithmetic of majority, but through the conscience of constitutional morality.
